explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the initialization process is completed successfully (initDone) or it fails (initFail) at any point in time (represented by bool_exp2), then eventually (F), the system must enter either the Standby Mode (StandbyMode) or the Out-of-Service Warning mode (OutOfServiceWarning). These modes serve as the explicit indications to the user about the outcome of the initialization (represented by bool_exp3). This LTL formula captures the requirement for the system to eventually communicate the initialization status to the user.

The property states that globally, if the system initialization starts (initStart), then eventually (F) the initialization process will either be completed successfully (initDone) or will have failed (initFail). This captures the requirement that the system shall reach a final state (either success or failure) after beginning the initialization, thereby 'indicating' its completion or failure, as there is no specific atomic proposition for 'indication to the user'.

The property states that eventually, the system will reach a state where the initialization process has either successfully completed (indicated by 'initDone') or has failed (indicated by 'initFail'). This directly captures the requirement that the system shall provide an indication of the initialization outcome.

The property states that eventually (F) in the future, the system will indicate that the initialization process has either been completed successfully (initDone) or has failed (initFail). This captures the requirement that one of these two outcomes must occur and be indicated to the user.

The property states that globally, if the system initialization starts, then eventually the initialization process will either be completed successfully or it will fail. This ensures that the initialization process always reaches a conclusive state (success or failure).

The property states that globally (G), whenever the system/controller initialization starts (bool_exp2), it must eventually (F) be completed successfully or fail (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that the initialization process must always terminate in either a success or failure state, which the system implicitly 'indicates' by reaching those states.

The LTL property states that globally, whenever the system/controller initialization starts (initStart), it will eventually either complete successfully (initDone) or fail (initFail). This ensures that the initialization process always reaches a definitive conclusion, which is then indicated to the user as per the requirement.

This property states that whenever the system's initialization process begins, it must eventually either successfully complete or fail, and this outcome will be indicated to the user. The atomic propositions 'initDone' and 'initFail' are understood to inherently represent this indication, as per their descriptions.

The property states that eventually, the system will indicate that the initialization process has either been completed successfully or failed. This is captured by the Finally (F) operator applied to a disjunction of 'initDone' (initialization success) and 'initFail' (initialization failure). The atomic propositions 'initDone' and 'initFail' themselves denote the indication of the status.

The property states that globally (G), if the system initialization starts (initStart), then eventually (F) the initialization process will either be completed successfully (initDone) or it will have failed (initFail). This captures the requirement that the system must indicate one of these two outcomes for the initialization process.
